title: dactylorhiza lapponica subsp. russowii
intro: Dactylorhiza lapponica subsp. russowii, also known as the Arctic marsh orchid, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Orchidaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Dactylorhiza lapponica subsp. russowii include reducing inflammation and improving respiratory health, as well as possessing potential anti-cancer properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions, although its use and preparation may vary depending on regional traditions and available research.
